title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and risk reporting rather than product releases. The strongest updates add evidence on frontier-risk monitoring, conflict-sensitive deployment failures, interpretability of model mistakes, privacy-preserving learning, and the security economics of model distillation—together pointing to a maturing stack for auditing how models fail, leak, and should be governed.

items.title: METR publishes Frontier Risk Report for February–March 2026
items.why_it_matters: Regular frontier-risk reporting helps operationalize preparedness, monitoring, and external scrutiny of advanced model capabilities.

items.title: Study tests whether LLM deployment can worsen armed conflicts
items.why_it_matters: It frames conflict-sensitive evaluation as a missing safety practice for real-world high-stakes deployments.

items.title: Sparse-feature audit links GPT-2 IOI failures to specific SAE activations
items.why_it_matters: Mechanistic audits of failure cases could make interpretability more useful for debugging model behavior.

items.title: Paper models adaptive distillation attacks and utility-preserving defenses
items.why_it_matters: It sharpens the security trade-off between helpful outputs and model extraction risk.

items.title: Lumberjack proposes stronger differentially private random forests
items.why_it_matters: Better privacy-utility trade-offs can make privacy-preserving ML more practical on sensitive tabular data.

items.title: LCGuard targets unsafe KV-cache sharing in multi-agent systems
items.why_it_matters: Guarding latent inter-agent communication addresses a less visible attack surface in agentic systems.

items.title: Commercial AI chatbots evaluated as news intermediaries across regions and languages
items.why_it_matters: Cross-lingual accuracy testing probes reliability risks as chatbots increasingly mediate public information.

items.title: OpenAI outlines content provenance work with credentials, SynthID, and verification tooling
items.why_it_matters: Provenance infrastructure is central to tracing synthetic media and improving transparency online.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
